cards.altText: Renault Zoe Q90 drive motor - electric 88hp replacement UK
knownFailures: Rotor position sensor fault - causes red warning message and no-start condition, common under 50k miles.

Motor bearing wear - requires 15+ hour casing split, documented on high-mileage pre-2017 examples.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Zoe Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Zoe's electric drive units are generally reliable, but the early Q90 motor (pre-August 2017) has well-documented failure patterns that dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt motor supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to understand your options.

problems.h4: Q90 Rotor Position Sensor Failure - Why Early Zoe Motors Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Renault Zoe Expression, Dynamique, Intens with Q90 motor (2013-2017, pre-August)
problems.rootCause: Faulty rotor position sensor sends incorrect data to the inverter, triggering a safety shutdown and red dashboard warning.

repairOptions.tier: Sensor Replacement Only - If caught early before cascade damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace rotor position sensor, reset ECU, diagnostic testing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if motor casing undamaged; risk of recurrence on original Q90 design

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Q90 Motor - Recommended for confirmed failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt motor with updated sensor, inverter check, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; rebuilt units include bearing and sensor upgrades

problems.recommendation: On a Zoe worth £6,000-£7,000, a £4,000 main dealer replacement is hard to justify. We recommend sourcing a rebuilt Q90 from a HEVRA-accredited specialist who can include bearing and sensor upgrades for roughly half the dealer cost.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Q90 motor prices for your Renault Zoe

problems.h4: Q90 Inverter Module Failure - Charging Faults & Reduced Power
problems.rootCause: Inverter module overheating or component fatigue disrupts power conversion, triggering limp mode or charging refusal.

repairOptions.tier: Inverter Repair/Replacement - If motor windings intact
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace or refurbish inverter module, software reset, load testing
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if motor itself is healthy; does not address bearing wear risk

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Q90 Motor - Recommended for confirmed cascade failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt motor with new inverter, full diagnostics, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; addresses all common Q90 failure points

problems.recommendation: If diagnostics confirm inverter failure alone, a specialist inverter repair can save money. But on high-mileage Q90 units, bearing wear often coexists - a full rebuilt motor may offer better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Q90 inverter or full motor replacement

problems.h4: Q90 Motor Bearing Wear - Knocking Noise & Catastrophic Failure Risk
problems.affectedModels: Renault Zoe Q90 (2013-2017, pre-August), high-mileage examples
problems.rootCause: Original bearing design wears prematurely; metal contamination damages rotor/stator if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Bearing Replacement Only - Specialist rebuild, motor intact
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Split motor casing (15+ hours), replace bearings, reseal, bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if rotor/stator undamaged; requires specialist EV workshop

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Q90 Motor - Recommended for confirmed damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit fully rebuilt motor with upgraded bearings, sensor, inverter check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if original motor shows signs of internal damage; includes all upgrades

problems.recommendation: Bearing noise on a Q90 is a serious warning. If caught early, a specialist bearing rebuild can extend life. But if knocking is loud or accompanied by error codes, a full rebuilt motor is often the safer, more cost-effective choice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Q90 motor prices with bearing upgrades

problems.h4: Charging System Faults - Rectifier & On-Board Charger Issues
problems.affectedModels: All Renault Zoe variants, Q90 and R-Series
problems.rootCause: On-board charger or rectifier module degradation reduces charging speed or prevents charging entirely.

repairOptions.tier: Charger Module Replacement - If motor/drive unit healthy
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace on-board charger or rectifier, software update, charging test
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated charging faults; does not address motor issues

repairOptions.tier: Full System Diagnostic + Motor Check - Recommended for multiple faults
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Comprehensive diagnostics, charger + motor inspection, prioritised repairs
repairOptions.longevity: Best for complex fault scenarios; ensures no hidden motor damage is missed

problems.recommendation: Charging faults don't always mean motor failure. Start with a specialist diagnostic to isolate the issue. If the motor is healthy, charger replacement alone may resolve the problem at lower cost.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault Zoe charging system or motor diagnostics

problems.group: Don't Let Motor Failure Write Off Your Renault Zoe

engineYears.h2: Renault Zoe Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major motor changes across the Zoe's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their drive unit and compare rebuilt motor prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Phase 1 Launch: Q90 Rapid Charge Dominates
years.description: Launch years featured the Q90 motor with 43kW rapid charge; early units show highest failure rates.
keyChanges: Renault Zoe launched in UK with Q90 88hp motor and 43kW rapid charge capability
Expression, Dynamique and Intens trims available with Q90 motor
Early production (pre-2015) shows highest concentration of rotor sensor failures
knownFor: Q90 motor failures dominate replacement enquiries for these years
Rotor position sensor faults trigger red dashboard warning and no-start
Bearing wear requires 15+ hour motor casing split on high-mileage examples
Battery warranty (8-year/100k miles) often still active on these models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2016 Renault Zoe replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Zoe Q90 motor failure • Zoe red warning message • Q90 bearing replacement cost

years.preview: Transition Year: Q90 to R90 Cutoff (August 2017)
years.description: Critical year: pre-August Q90 units retain failure risks; post-August R90 motors show improved reliability.
keyChanges: August 2017 production cutoff: Q90 (rapid charge) replaced by R90 (standard charge)
R90 motor introduced with 22kW charging, improved bearing and sensor design
Intens and Zen trims available with either motor depending on build date
knownFor: Verify build date: pre-August 2017 = Q90 failure risk; post-August = R90 reliability
Q90 to R90 swap requires different charging hardware and ECU configuration
R90 units show significantly lower failure rates in UK owner forums
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017 Renault Zoe replacement engines
years.ticker: Q90 vs R90 Zoe motor • August 2017 Zoe motor change • Zoe charging hardware compatibility

years.preview: Phase 1 Facelift: R90 and R110 Standardisation
years.description: R90 and R110 motors standardised across Phase 1 facelift; reliability improves, Q90 phased out.
keyChanges: R90 (92hp) and R110 (108hp) motors become standard across most trims
Q90 motor discontinued for new UK registrations
Zen, Business and Signature trims introduced with R-Series motors
knownFor: R-Series motors show minimal failure reports in UK owner forums
R110 offers noticeable power increase for motorway driving
22kW standard charging sufficient for most home/public charging needs

years.preview: Phase 2 Facelift: R110 and R135 Performance Era
years.description: Phase 2 facelift introduced refreshed styling, R110 standard and R135 performance motor for GT variants.
keyChanges: Phase 2 facelift launched with updated exterior/interior and R110/R135 motors
GT Line, GT and R.S. Line trims feature R135 134hp performance motor
All Phase 2 models use 22kW standard charging; rapid charge via separate option
mainEngines: R135 - 134hp electric (GT Line, GT, R.S. Line)
knownFor: R135 motor delivers strong performance with no widespread failure reports
Phase 2 models benefit from updated software and thermal management
Late-model Zoes often still under original battery warranty
years.cta: Enquire about Renault Zoe 2019-2024 engine availability

faq.h2: Renault Zoe Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Why do Renault Zoe Q90 motors fail so often?
items.answer: Early Q90 motors (pre-August 2017) have documented weaknesses in rotor position sensors and bearings that cause failures under 50k miles. Rebuilt units with upgraded components address these design flaws.
keyPoints: Rotor position sensor faults trigger red dashboard warnings and no-start conditions
Bearing wear requires 15+ hour motor casing split on high-mileage examples
Inverter module failures often cascade from sensor or bearing issues
Rebuilt Q90 motors include updated sensors and bearings to prevent recurrence
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Q90 motor prices for your Renault Zoe

items.question: How much does a Renault Zoe motor replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Main dealers charge £3,800-£5,000+ for Q90 replacement; independent HEVRA specialists offer rebuilt units for £1,900-£2,800 including upgraded components and 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: Q90 rebuilt motors: £1,900-£4,500 supply only from specialists
R90/R110 rebuilt motors: £2,200-£5,500 depending on variant
Supply & fit service adds £800-£1,500 depending on location
Always verify motor code (Q90 vs R90) before ordering replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Zoe motor replacement from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Renault Zoe motor or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: On a Zoe worth £6,000-£7,000, a £4,000 dealer replacement is hard to justify, but a £1,900-£2,800 specialist rebuild often makes financial sense for continued ownership.
keyPoints: Compare rebuild cost (£1,900-£2,800) vs car value (£6,000-£7,000)
Factor in remaining battery warranty (8-year/100k miles) which adds value
Specialist rebuilds include upgraded components addressing original failure points
Consider selling as non-runner vs investing in reliable rebuilt motor
items.cta: Find out if your Zoe is worth repairing with a free quote comparison

items.question: Can I replace a Q90 motor with an R90 or R110?
items.answer: Swapping Q90 for R90/R110 requires different charging hardware (43kW rapid vs 22kW standard) and ECU reconfiguration; not a simple plug-and-play upgrade.
keyPoints: Q90 supports 43kW rapid charge; R90/R110 use 22kW standard charging only
Charging port, onboard charger and ECU must be matched to motor code
Specialist EV workshops can perform conversions but cost may exceed rebuild
Most owners find rebuilding the original Q90 more cost-effective than swapping
items.cta: Get expert advice on Q90 to R90 conversion feasibility for your Zoe

items.question: Where can I find a specialist for Renault Zoe motor repair?
items.answer: HEVRA-accredited workshops like Cleevely EV specialise in Zoe motor rebuilds, offering bearing replacements and sensor upgrades at roughly half main dealer cost.
keyPoints: HEVRA (Hybrid & Electric Vehicle Repair Alliance) certifies qualified EV specialists
Independent specialists can split motor casings and replace bearings cost-effectively
Always request warranty terms and component upgrade details before committing
EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Zoe specialists for quote comparison
items.cta: Find a HEVRA-accredited Renault Zoe specialist near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Renault Zoe motor?
items.answer: Rebuilt motors from EngineMarket suppliers include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for the replacement unit.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt units from vetted UK suppliers
Warranty covers motor internals, sensors and bearings installed during rebuild
Always confirm exact terms, exclusions and claim process with chosen supplier
Supply & fit service often includes additional labour warranty from installer
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on rebuilt Renault Zoe motors before you buy

items.question: How long does a Renault Zoe motor replacement take?
items.answer: Specialist workshops typically complete motor replacement in 1-3 days depending on parts availability; bearing-only rebuilds may take longer due to 15+ hour casing split labour.
keyPoints: Full motor swap: 1-2 days labour once unit is in stock
Bearing rebuild: 3-5 days due to complex disassembly/reassembly process
Supply & fit service includes diagnostics, calibration and road testing
Nationwide delivery options can reduce downtime if local specialist unavailable
items.cta: Get a timeline estimate for your Renault Zoe motor replacement

items.question: Are used Renault Zoe motors reliable?
items.answer: Used Q90 motors carry the same design flaws as the original; used R90/R110/R135 units are generally reliable but require verification of mileage, error history and motor code compatibility.
keyPoints: Used Q90 motors may still fail due to original sensor/bearing design
Always request diagnostic report and mileage verification before purchase
R-Series used motors (R90/R110/R135) show better reliability track record
Rebuilt units with upgraded components often offer better long-term value than used
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Renault Zoe motor options and pricing

items.question: Do I need a specialist to fit a Renault Zoe replacement motor?
items.answer: Yes - high-voltage safety training, specialist tools and ECU calibration are required; DIY fitting risks injury, voids warranty and may damage the vehicle.
keyPoints: EV motor replacement requires high-voltage isolation and safety procedures
ECU coding and charging system calibration need specialist diagnostic equipment
HEVRA-accredited workshops ensure compliance with EV safety standards
Supply & fit service includes post-installation testing and warranty validation
items.cta: Find a qualified Renault Zoe motor fitting specialist in your area

items.question: What's the difference between Renault Zoe Q90 and R90 motors?
items.answer: Q90 offers 43kW rapid charge but has documented failure risks; R90 has improved reliability with 22kW standard charging only - motor code determines charging hardware compatibility.

items.question: 88hp, 43kW rapid charge, pre-August 2017, higher failure rate
items.cta: Compare Q90 vs R90 Renault Zoe motor prices and compatibility
